nodes.languageNotes: Resolves both the per-worktree git-dir and shared git-common-dir as watch targets; is_interesting_fs_event filters notify events to relevant change kinds before debounced re-emission.

nodes.name: worktrees.rs
nodes.summary: Git worktree lifecycle commands: list/add/remove worktrees, orphan/markerless cleanup, prune, and branch enumeration, with a pure porcelain parser and gitdir-marker normalization for managed worktrees.
nodes.languageNotes: parse_worktrees_porcelain reads git worktree list --porcelain into Worktree records (main flag, locked, created_at); remove_orphan and is_managed_markerless_worktree recover from worktrees whose gitdir marker has been deleted.

nodes.name: GhCheckRun
nodes.summary: Serde data model for a single GitHub Actions check run (name, status, conclusion, URL) surfaced to the frontend.

nodes.name: GhChecks
nodes.summary: Aggregate CI rollup payload (availability, repo, overall conclusion, pass/fail/pending/skipped counts, and the per-run list) returned by gh_checks.

nodes.name: gh_checks
nodes.summary: Tauri command wrapper that returns the best-effort GitHub check-run rollup for a project branch.

nodes.name: gh_checks_inner
nodes.summary: Resolves the repo, verifies gh auth, fetches check-runs for a branch via the gh API, and assembles the GhChecks rollup; the testable core behind the gh_checks command.

nodes.name: parse_check_runs_json
nodes.summary: Pure parser turning the GitHub check-runs JSON response into per-run records plus aggregate counts, with URL fallback handling.

nodes.name: rollup_conclusion
nodes.summary: Collapses a list of check conclusions into a single dominant verdict (failure > pending > success > neutral).

nodes.name: read_only_git_command
nodes.summary: Builds a PATH-resolved git command configured to avoid optional index locks during background reads.

nodes.name: read_only_gh_command
nodes.summary: Builds a PATH-resolved gh command with the same optional-lock guard applied for child git operations.

nodes.name: read_only_tokio_gh_command
nodes.summary: Builds an async gh command with optional-lock suppression for tokio-based GitHub queries.

nodes.name: fail_if_index_locked
nodes.summary: Rejects a background git read when a recent index.lock exists so Aethon does not race active git operations.

nodes.name: read_only_git_dir
nodes.summary: Resolves the absolute .git directory for a path using a read-only rev-parse, returning None outside a repo.

nodes.name: DiffHunk
nodes.summary: Serializable line-range description for one added, modified, or deleted hunk in new-file coordinates.

nodes.name: DiffStat
nodes.summary: Serializable insertion and deletion count pair for aggregate or per-file diffs.

nodes.name: rel_to_active
nodes.summary: Normalizes a frontend-supplied file path into a git pathspec relative to the active project root.

nodes.name: git_file_diff_hunks
nodes.summary: Returns parsed working-tree-versus-HEAD hunk ranges for one file so Monaco can render dirty gutter markers.

nodes.name: git_file_diff
nodes.summary: Returns unified diff text for one file relative to HEAD, degrading to an empty diff for unresolved or untracked files.

nodes.name: git_diff_stat
nodes.summary: Aggregates insertion and deletion counts for the active root by summing git --numstat output.

nodes.name: git_file_diff_stat
nodes.summary: Computes insertion and deletion counts for a single file relative to HEAD.

nodes.name: parse_numstat
nodes.summary: Sums numeric add/delete columns from git --numstat output while ignoring binary-file placeholders.

nodes.name: git_show_head
nodes.summary: Reads the file content at HEAD for the original side of a diff view.

nodes.name: parse_diff_hunks
nodes.summary: Parses zero-context git diff hunk headers into added, modified, and deleted line ranges.

nodes.name: parse_range
nodes.summary: Parses one old-file or new-file hunk range token into a start and count pair.

nodes.name: GhBranchStatus
nodes.summary: Serde payload describing a branch's remote push state, associated PRs, and whether its worktree is broken.

nodes.name: GhPr
nodes.summary: Serde model for a pull request summary (number, state, title, URL, draft/merged flags, base ref).

nodes.name: GhRepoOverview
nodes.summary: Serde payload aggregating repository metadata (description, URL, default branch, stars, forks, open issue/PR counts, last push) for the dashboard.

nodes.name: RepoViewParts
nodes.summary: Internal parsed subset of gh repo view output used to test repository metadata parsing separately from shell execution.

nodes.name: gh_branch_status
nodes.summary: Tauri command wrapper that fetches GitHub branch push and pull request state for a worktree branch.

nodes.name: worktree_is_dangling
nodes.summary: Detects a broken worktree whose gitdir marker no longer resolves, so gh calls can be short-circuited rather than erroring.

nodes.name: normalize_path
nodes.summary: Lexically normalizes a path (resolving . and .. without touching disk) for worktree-marker comparison.

nodes.name: gh_branch_status_inner
nodes.summary: Resolves the repo, checks gh auth, queries remote branch existence and open PRs for a branch, and assembles GhBranchStatus; the testable core of gh_branch_status.

nodes.name: url_encode_path_segment
nodes.summary: Percent-encodes a path segment (notably slash-bearing branch names) for safe interpolation into gh API routes.

nodes.name: parse_repo_view_json
nodes.summary: Pure parser mapping the gh repo view JSON into the RepoViewParts struct consumed by the overview builder.

nodes.name: gh_repo_overview
nodes.summary: Tauri command wrapper that returns best-effort repository overview metrics.

nodes.name: gh_repo_overview_inner
nodes.summary: Fetches repo view metadata plus open PR and issue counts via concurrent gh calls and builds the GhRepoOverview payload.

nodes.name: gh_repo_avatar_url
nodes.summary: Tauri command that resolves the owner's avatar URL for a repository via the gh API.

nodes.name: GhIssue
nodes.summary: Serde model for an issue summary row (number, title, URL, state, labels, updated time, author, comment count).

nodes.name: GhIssueLabel
nodes.summary: Serializable GitHub issue label chip data with optional color.

nodes.name: GhIssueDetail
nodes.summary: Serde model for a single issue's full detail (number, title, URL, body, author).

nodes.name: parse_gh_issue_list
nodes.summary: Pure parser converting gh issue list JSON into GhIssue records, extracting labels and author fields.

nodes.name: gh_issue_list
nodes.summary: Tauri command that shells out to gh issue list for open issues and returns the parsed records.

nodes.name: gh_issue_view
nodes.summary: Tauri command that fetches a single issue's detail via gh issue view and returns a GhIssueDetail.

nodes.name: pick_project_directory
nodes.summary: Opens a native folder-selection dialog and returns the chosen project directory path.

nodes.name: GitFetchState
nodes.summary: Managed state for deduplicating background fetch attempts by repository common directory.

nodes.name: GitStatus
nodes.summary: Serde model for the high-level status summary (branch, dirty flag, ahead/behind counts).

nodes.name: GitFileStatusEntry
nodes.summary: Serde model for one changed file's path, status code, and optional original (rename source) path.

nodes.name: GitWorkingContext
nodes.summary: Serde model summarizing a workspace's repo root, branch, worktree flag, changed-file count, and ahead/behind for git-context callers.

nodes.name: git_fetch_all
nodes.summary: Tauri command wrapper that starts a deduplicated background fetch of all remotes for a project repository.

nodes.name: git_fetch_all_inner
nodes.summary: Runs git fetch --all for a project, coordinating with GitFetchState to throttle concurrent fetch attempts per repo.

nodes.name: is_inside_work_tree
nodes.summary: Checks whether a directory is inside a git worktree via rev-parse.

nodes.name: git_common_dir
nodes.summary: Resolves the shared git common directory used to deduplicate fetch attempts across linked worktrees.

nodes.name: reserve_attempt
nodes.summary: Records and deduplicates recent fetch attempts under a mutex-protected per-repository key.

nodes.name: git_status
nodes.summary: Primary Tauri command producing the branch/dirty/ahead/behind status summary from porcelain output, guarding against a locked index.

nodes.name: git_working_context
nodes.summary: Resolves the active root, branch, worktree flag, and change counts into a GitWorkingContext for a cwd.

nodes.name: git_rev_parse_abs_path
nodes.summary: Resolves and canonicalizes git paths such as --git-dir and --git-common-dir for worktree comparison.

nodes.name: read_head_branch
nodes.summary: Reads the current symbolic branch or falls back to a short commit SHA for detached HEAD.

nodes.name: read_branch_porcelain_counts
nodes.summary: Reads git porcelain branch output and delegates parsing of changed, ahead, and behind counts.

nodes.name: parse_branch_porcelain_counts
nodes.summary: Pure parser extracting ahead/behind counts from the porcelain --branch header line.

nodes.name: git_file_status
nodes.summary: Tauri command producing the per-file changed-path breakdown from porcelain v2 -z output, rebased to the active root.

nodes.name: resolve_repo_and_active_root
nodes.summary: Resolves both the repository root and the active worktree root for a directory so status reporting stays worktree-scoped.

nodes.name: git_ignored_paths
nodes.summary: Tauri command enumerating gitignored paths under a root via git status --ignored porcelain output.

nodes.name: parse_nul_paths
nodes.summary: Decodes a NUL-delimited git byte stream into non-empty path strings.

nodes.name: path_relative_to_active_root
nodes.summary: Converts repository-relative git paths into paths relative to the active root.

nodes.name: classify_git_status
nodes.summary: Maps a porcelain XY status pair to a normalized file-status classification for the frontend.

nodes.name: parse_git_file_status_porcelain_z
nodes.summary: Pure parser splitting NUL-delimited porcelain v2 output into GitFileStatusEntry records, handling rename source/destination pairs.

nodes.name: init_repo
nodes.summary: Initializes a git repo with a deterministic identity and initial commit at the given path for tests.

nodes.name: GitWatchState
nodes.summary: Managed Tauri state holding active filesystem watchers keyed by repo root.

nodes.name: resolve_git_watch_targets
nodes.summary: Resolves the set of paths (git-dir and git-common-dir) that should be watched for a repo root.

nodes.name: git_watch_root
nodes.summary: Tauri command that installs a debounced filesystem watcher on a repo's git targets and emits GitStateChanged events on interesting changes.

nodes.name: git_unwatch_root
nodes.summary: Tauri command that tears down and removes the filesystem watcher for a repo root.

nodes.name: Worktree
nodes.summary: Serde model describing a git worktree (path, branch, head, main flag, locked state, created-at timestamp).

nodes.name: BranchInfo
nodes.summary: Serde model for a branch listing entry (name plus current-branch flag).

nodes.name: git_worktrees
nodes.summary: Tauri command listing all worktrees for a project via git worktree list --porcelain and parsing them into Worktree records.

nodes.name: parse_worktrees_porcelain
nodes.summary: Pure parser turning git worktree list --porcelain output into Worktree records, deriving main/locked flags and a created-at timestamp.

nodes.name: git_worktree_add
nodes.summary: Tauri command creating a new worktree (existing or new branch from a base) and returning the refreshed worktree list.

nodes.name: git_worktree_remove
nodes.summary: Tauri command removing a worktree (optionally forced) and returning the refreshed list.

nodes.name: git_worktree_remove_orphan
nodes.summary: Recovers and removes a worktree whose gitdir marker has been deleted, including managed markerless directories, then prunes stale administrative entries.

nodes.name: is_managed_markerless_worktree
nodes.summary: Recognizes leftover worktree directories inside Aethon or Claude managed worktree roots.

nodes.name: normalize_path
nodes.summary: Lexically normalizes paths by resolving current-directory and parent-directory components without requiring filesystem existence.

nodes.name: git_branch_list
nodes.summary: Tauri command enumerating local branches via for-each-ref and flagging the current branch.

nodes.name: issue-task.ts
nodes.summary: Builds agent task payloads from GitHub issues: assembles the issue prompt, derives a conventional-commit-style git branch (with collision uniquification and length compaction), and interpolates per-project issue templates.

nodes.name: issue-templates.ts
nodes.summary: Loads and normalizes per-project issue-task templates from disk via a Tauri invoke, and selects the templates matching a given issue's labels.

nodes.name: issues-section.test.ts
nodes.summary: Exercises the GitHub issues dashboard UI and helper behavior, including branch generation, template loading, send-to-agent events, session reuse, and context-menu actions.

nodes.name: issues-section.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the dashboard GitHub issues section, lazy-loads issue data and templates, opens issues in the browser, and dispatches issue-backed `start-task` events to the agent workflow.
nodes.languageNotes: React hooks coordinate lazy IntersectionObserver loading, async IPC-backed cache refreshes, and event dispatch while keeping project data ref-addressable.

nodes.name: ghIssuesCache.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the GitHub issues cache verifying TTL behavior, in-flight de-duplication, force refresh, and error fallback against a mocked Tauri invoke.

nodes.name: ghIssuesCache.ts
nodes.summary: In-memory TTL cache (90s live, 60s negative) over the gh_issue_list Tauri command, with in-flight request de-duplication, force-refresh, peek, and per-issue detail fetch.
nodes.languageNotes: De-duplicates concurrent fetches via an in-flight promise map keyed by project+limit.

nodes.name: time.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for formatRelativeTime covering the now, minutes, hours, days, and locale-date boundaries.

nodes.name: time.ts
nodes.summary: Provides formatRelativeTime, converting an epoch-millisecond timestamp into a human-friendly relative string (now, minutes, hours, days) or a locale date.

nodes.name: buildIssuePrompt
nodes.summary: Composes a Markdown prompt instructing the agent to work on a specific GitHub issue, embedding the number, title, URL, author, and body.

nodes.name: buildIssueTask
nodes.summary: Top-level task assembler that selects a template, derives a unique branch, and interpolates branch/prompt templates to produce the structured task forwarded to the agent.

nodes.name: interpolateIssueTemplate
nodes.summary: Replaces {{var}} placeholders in a template string with values from the supplied variables map.

nodes.name: issueTemplateVariables
nodes.summary: Builds the variable map exposed to issue templates, including issue metadata, derived branch, slug, labels, and project context.

nodes.name: buildIssueBranch
nodes.summary: Derives a git branch name from an issue (type prefix + slug), compacting overlong names and falling back to a timestamp suffix when collisions persist.

nodes.name: uniquifyIssueBranch
nodes.summary: Adds numeric or timestamp suffixes to a requested issue branch when the name already exists.

nodes.name: compactIssueBranch
nodes.summary: Truncates a branch name to a maximum length while protecting the prefix and trimming on a word boundary where possible.

nodes.name: classifyIssueBranch
nodes.summary: Parses a conventional-commit-style scope/type from the issue title and combines it with a label-derived prefix to classify the branch.

nodes.name: branchPrefixFor
nodes.summary: Maps a normalized commit type token to the canonical git branch prefix (feat, fix, docs, chore, etc.).

nodes.name: branchPrefixForLabels
nodes.summary: Infers a branch prefix from issue labels by regex-matching label names against bug, docs, ci, test, perf, refactor, chore, and feature categories.

nodes.name: loadIssueTemplates
nodes.summary: Reads the project's issue-template config through a Tauri invoke and normalizes it, returning an empty config with a warning on failure.

nodes.name: normalizeIssueTemplatesConfig
nodes.summary: Coerces raw config input into a validated templates array plus optional warning, dropping malformed template entries.

nodes.name: normalizeIssueTemplate
nodes.summary: Validates a single raw template object, trimming fields and filtering label conditions, returning null when required fields are missing.

nodes.name: matchingIssueTemplates
nodes.summary: Returns templates applicable to an issue, ordering label-specific matches ahead of catch-all templates.

nodes.name: renderIssues
nodes.summary: Sets up mocked issue data, template IPC, project workspace state, and renders IssuesSection for dashboard workflow tests.

nodes.name: resolveOrInline
nodes.summary: Resolves JSON-pointer-backed component props against app state while allowing inline project objects.

nodes.name: projectWorkspaceBranches
nodes.summary: Collects branch and label strings for the active project workspaces so new issue task branches can avoid collisions.

nodes.name: currentProjectWorkspaceId
nodes.summary: Finds the active workspace id for the project using global activeWorkspaceId first and sidebar active flags as fallback.

nodes.name: workspaceLabelForIssueSession
nodes.summary: Builds the display label for an existing issue session from branch, workspace metadata, path basename, or project-root fallback.

nodes.name: issueSessionStatus
nodes.summary: Detects whether an issue already has a linked agent tab and summarizes its running, attention, or session state for the row badge.

nodes.name: IssuesSection
nodes.summary: Coordinates issue lazy-loading, template loading, row rendering, context-menu actions, browser opening, and start-task dispatch for GitHub issue workflows.

nodes.name: makeIssue
nodes.summary: Test factory producing a GhIssue fixture with overridable fields.

nodes.name: getIssues
nodes.summary: Returns cached issues when fresh, otherwise fetches via gh_issue_list, coalescing concurrent calls and caching empty results on error to avoid hammering gh.

nodes.name: refreshIssues
nodes.summary: Evicts the cache entry for a project/limit and re-fetches issues, forcing a fresh gh call.

nodes.name: peekIssues
nodes.summary: Reads a cached issue list for a project and limit without triggering an IPC fetch.

nodes.name: clearAllIssues
nodes.summary: Clears every cached GitHub issue entry for the global dashboard refresh path.

nodes.name: getIssueDetail
nodes.summary: Fetches full detail for a single issue number via the gh_issue_detail Tauri command.

nodes.name: __clearCacheForTesting
nodes.summary: Clears cache and in-flight state so tests can start from a deterministic cache boundary.

nodes.name: formatRelativeTime
nodes.summary: Formats an epoch-millisecond timestamp as a relative time string, falling back to a locale date string beyond a week.

nodes.name: io.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite exercising the local-chat append/prune/truncate I/O helpers, asserting the on-disk JSONL chat file stays bounded and consistent.

nodes.name: io.ts
nodes.summary: Handles disk IO for session labels and local chat JSONL, including message normalization, pruning, and transcript truncation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses async filesystem operations with append-and-rewrite helpers to keep local chat history bounded and recoverable.

nodes.name: normalizeSessionLabel
nodes.summary: Normalizes user-provided session labels by trimming and enforcing the configured maximum label length.

nodes.name: readSessionLabel
nodes.summary: Reads the optional persisted session label file and returns a bounded display label when present.

nodes.name: writeSessionLabel
nodes.summary: Creates or removes the session label file depending on whether the normalized label is empty.

nodes.name: appendLocalChatMessage
nodes.summary: Appends a normalized local chat entry with text, attachments, A2UI payload, cwd, and timestamp, then prunes the file to the retention limit.

nodes.name: serializeLocalChatEntry
nodes.summary: Serializes a local chat entry while omitting undefined optional fields so rewritten JSONL stays compact.

nodes.name: pruneLocalChatFile
nodes.summary: Reads local chat JSONL, keeps the newest retained entries, and rewrites the file when pruning is needed.

nodes.name: truncateLocalChatAfterEntry
nodes.summary: Removes local chat entries after a cutoff timestamp so restored history can be trimmed after rollback points.

nodes.name: lookup.ts
nodes.summary: Locates the pi session log whose recorded cwd matches an expected project directory, canonicalizing paths via realpath and reading only the session-header line for efficiency.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads just the header bytes via a fixed-size buffer (open/read/close) rather than loading the whole session log, with a realpath cache keyed on canonical cwd.

nodes.name: canonicalCwd
nodes.summary: Resolves a path to its canonical form via realpath with trailing-slash stripping and a memoization cache, falling back to the lexical path on error.

nodes.name: readSessionHeaderCwd
nodes.summary: Reads just the leading bytes of a session log and parses the header line to extract the recorded cwd without loading the entire file.

nodes.name: findSessionFileMatchingCwd
nodes.summary: Scans a session directory for the session log whose canonicalized header cwd matches an expected directory, returning the most recent match.

nodes.name: metadata.ts
nodes.summary: Derives session summary metadata (cwd, first-message preview, label, validity) from the latest session log in a session directory for listing recent sessions.

nodes.name: metaFromSessionLines
nodes.summary: Extracts cwd and a first-user-message preview from raw session log lines to build summary metadata for a session.

nodes.name: latestSessionLog
nodes.summary: Finds the most recently modified session log file within a session directory.

nodes.name: readSessionMetadata
nodes.summary: Assembles a session's summary metadata from its latest log plus stored label, validating that the recorded cwd still exists as a directory.

nodes.name: parse-local.ts
nodes.summary: Parses Aethon local chat JSONL into restored messages while filtering invalid roles, mismatched cwd entries, and duplicate tool-card records.

nodes.name: firstSeenToolCardIndex
nodes.summary: Finds whether any tool-card identity in a local message has already appeared, supporting duplicate replacement semantics.

nodes.name: parseLocalChatLines
nodes.summary: Parses local chat JSONL into restored messages, validates roles and cwd, trims content, parses attachments, and deduplicates tool cards.

nodes.name: readLocalChatTranscript
nodes.summary: Reads the local chat file for a session and delegates line parsing with optional cwd filtering.

nodes.name: restore.ts
nodes.summary: Orchestrates session restoration by merging pi transcripts with local chat records, preserving attachments, file-change tool cards, cancellations, and chronological order.
nodes.languageNotes: The module decomposes a large restore pipeline into small pure helpers for content matching, tool-card identity reconciliation, attachment merging, and ordering.

nodes.name: messageContentParts
nodes.summary: Extracts normalized text and thinking fragments from a restored message for content-based comparison.

nodes.name: piContentIndex
nodes.summary: Builds an index of pi transcript message content parts so local messages can be matched against existing pi content.

nodes.name: isCoveredByPiContent
nodes.summary: Determines whether a local message is already represented by pi content using id, timestamp, and content overlap checks.

nodes.name: fileChangeRichness
nodes.summary: Scores file-change records by the richness of path, diff, before, and after data to choose the best recovered payload.

nodes.name: localToolFileChanges
nodes.summary: Collects the richest local file-change records keyed by tool-card identity for later merge into pi tool cards.

nodes.name: mergeFileChange
nodes.summary: Merges local and pi file-change records while preserving richer diff and before/after content from either side.

nodes.name: mergeLocalFileChangesIntoPiMessages
nodes.summary: Patches pi tool-card messages with richer local file-change details when identities line up.

nodes.name: cancelledToolCardIdentitySet
nodes.summary: Extracts tool-card identities from local cancelled messages so redundant pi tool-card output can be removed.

nodes.name: isCoveredByPiToolCard
nodes.summary: Checks whether a local tool-card message is already covered by pi tool-card identities, with special handling for subagent tools.

nodes.name: removePiToolCardsCoveredByLocalCancellation
nodes.summary: Filters pi tool-card messages that are superseded by local cancellation records.

nodes.name: isCompactionMarker
nodes.summary: Recognizes compacted-history marker messages by normalized leading text.

nodes.name: isCoveredByPiCompaction
nodes.summary: Detects local compaction markers that are already represented by nearby pi compaction messages.

nodes.name: dedupeLocalMessages
nodes.summary: Filters local restored messages already covered by pi ids, compaction markers, tool cards, or content matches.

nodes.name: attachmentKey
nodes.summary: Builds a stable key from attachment metadata to compare restored attachments across local and pi messages.

nodes.name: mergeAttachments
nodes.summary: Combines pi and local attachments while preserving order and avoiding duplicate attachment metadata.

nodes.name: sameRestoredContent
nodes.summary: Compares restored message text and thinking parts to determine whether two messages carry the same content.

nodes.name: messageTimeDistance
nodes.summary: Computes timestamp distance between a local and pi message, treating missing timestamps as non-matches.

nodes.name: findPiMessageForLocalAttachments
nodes.summary: Finds the closest pi message with matching content so local attachments can be merged into it.

nodes.name: mergeLocalAttachmentsIntoPiMessages
nodes.summary: Copies local-only attachments into matching pi messages without duplicating existing attachment records.

nodes.name: mergeRestoredMessagesChronologically
nodes.summary: Combines pi and local messages and sorts them by creation time while preserving stable original order ties.

nodes.name: readSessionTranscript
nodes.summary: Restores the full session transcript by locating pi logs, reading local chat, merging tool-card/file-change details, deduping, and limiting restored messages.

nodes.name: shared.ts
nodes.summary: Defines shared session-history constants, restored message types, and parsing helpers for chat content, attachments, cwd normalization, and tool-card identities.

nodes.name: isChatRole
nodes.summary: Checks whether a value is one of the supported chat roles used in restored session messages.

nodes.name: hasA2ui
nodes.summary: Determines whether a parsed local chat record carries a non-empty A2UI payload.

nodes.name: normalizeToolCallId
nodes.summary: Normalizes tool call identifiers into bounded, filesystem- and id-safe tokens.

nodes.name: toolCardIdentityFromId
nodes.summary: Derives a stable tool-card identity from component ids or generated tool id patterns.

nodes.name: toolCardRecordsFromA2ui
nodes.summary: Walks A2UI payload nodes and extracts tool-card identity, status, and order metadata.

nodes.name: parseChatAttachments
nodes.summary: Converts raw attachment-like records into normalized chat attachment objects with known scalar fields.

nodes.name: trimText
nodes.summary: Trims message text to the maximum stored length while preserving undefined for empty content.

nodes.name: normalizeCwd
nodes.summary: Normalizes cwd strings by removing trailing slashes and returning undefined for blank values.

nodes.name: textFromContent
nodes.summary: Extracts visible text from string or structured content arrays in pi transcript messages.

nodes.name: thinkingFromContent
nodes.summary: Extracts thinking text from structured content arrays in pi transcript messages.

nodes.name: _ci.yml
nodes.summary: Reusable workflow_call CI gate defining the single source-of-truth lint, typecheck, and test jobs (TS, Playwright e2e, Rust) consumed by ci.yml and release-please.yml.
nodes.languageNotes: Reusable workflow invoked via `on: workflow_call`; runs no secrets so callers omit `secrets: inherit`. ts and rust jobs use an os matrix (ubuntu-24.04 + macos-15).

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: Top-level CI trigger that fires on push/PR to main and delegates all checks to the reusable _ci.yml workflow, with cancel-in-progress concurrency per ref.
nodes.languageNotes: Thin caller — its single job uses `./.github/workflows/_ci.yml`, keeping the check definitions in exactly one place.

nodes.name: copilot-review.yml
nodes.summary: Requests a GitHub Copilot review on every non-draft PR by assigning the Copilot reviewer via REST and posting an `@copilot review` comment as a reliable trigger.
nodes.languageNotes: Belt-and-braces dual trigger: REST `requested_reviewers` plus a PR comment, since the REST assignment alone has not reliably activated Copilot.

nodes.name: docs.yml
nodes.summary: Documentation workflow that builds the VitePress website for docs-related changes and deploys the generated site to GitHub Pages from main.
nodes.languageNotes: Separates Pages build and deploy jobs with branch and event guards.

nodes.name: nightly.yml
nodes.summary: Nightly macOS build pipeline chained off green CI via workflow_run; computes a dev version, builds a signed Tauri bundle into a staging release, then atomically promotes staging to the live nightly release.
nodes.languageNotes: Triggered by `workflow_run` after the CI workflow succeeds on main; resolves the tested head_sha explicitly. Build has a 3-attempt continue-on-error retry pattern to absorb Apple-notarization flakes; publish promotes nightly-staging → nightly with a ~1-2s blackout window.

nodes.name: release-please.yml
nodes.summary: Release automation that runs release-please to manage the release PR, re-syncs version files, re-runs the CI gate, builds and signs the macOS Tauri bundle, publishes the GitHub release, and publishes the aethon crate to crates.io.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a short-lived GitHub App token so the release PR is authored by a real identity and CI fires (default GITHUB_TOKEN would be suppressed by GitHub's recursion guard). Embeds the reusable _ci.yml as a gate before the signed build; build uses the same 3-attempt notarization retry.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build-context exclusion list that keeps node_modules, build artifacts, target/, and local state out of the image build, used by the AUR/packaging Dockerfile.

nodes.name: .release-please-manifest.json
nodes.summary: Pins the current release-please version state for the root package so automated stable and nightly release workflows can derive the correct next version.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Provides the repository operating manual for coding agents, covering the Aethon stack, architecture boundaries, testing discipline, devshell commands, event routing, terminal model, voice, auth, and release conventions.
nodes.languageNotes: Large Markdown guide organized as an agent-facing architecture and workflow reference with many second- and third-level sections.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Records Aethon release history with release-please generated sections for shipped features, fixes, refactors, tests, and build changes across versions.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project guidance for Claude Code covering the Tauri/React/agent stack, common commands, the three-layer architecture, frontend model, agent process concurrency, devshell wrapping, and Nix build notes.

nodes.name: DESIGN.md
nodes.summary: Aethon design system specification defining the Ember Brass color palette, typography hierarchy, elevation/shadow vocabulary, component recipes, and the signature agent-canvas and terminal surfaces.

nodes.name: PRODUCT.md
nodes.summary: Product brief describing the target users, product purpose, brand personality, anti-references, design principles, and accessibility goals for Aethon.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Public project overview for Aethon, explaining the Tauri, React, Bun, pi, and A2UI architecture, major capabilities, setup commands, versioning flow, project layout, and release pointers.

nodes.name: RELEASING.md
nodes.summary: Release operations guide detailing the stable and nightly release flows, Tauri signing key and Apple notarization one-time setup, local signed builds, and CI signing verification.

nodes.name: SPEC.md
nodes.summary: Design specification and milestone checklist (M1 through M7) capturing the vision, layered architecture, A2UI integration, extension model, theming, packaging, and configuration decisions for Aethon.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry point that mounts the React app into #root via /src/main.tsx and preloads the Geist, Inter, JetBrains Mono, and Playfair Display web fonts.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node package manifest for the Aethon frontend, CLI, agent bridge, and build/test tooling, including scripts for Vite, Tauri, Vitest, Playwright, ESLint, typecheck, and version synchronization.

nodes.name: release-please-config.json
nodes.summary: release-please configuration setting the release-type, pre-major minor bumping, custom changelog sections, and the tracked package list for automated release PRs.

nodes.name: rust-toolchain.toml
nodes.summary: Rust toolchain pin for non-Nix builds; the Nix devshell pin in flake.nix overrides this inside the dev environment.

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project-reference config for the React frontend source, compiling src/** with bundler module resolution and strict settings for the Vite app.

nodes.name: tsconfig.e2e.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project-reference config scoped to the Playwright end-to-end test sources under e2e/.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root TypeScript solution config that owns no files and composes the app, node, and e2e project references for tsc -b build mode.
nodes.languageNotes: Solution-style tsconfig: empty files array plus references array drives composite project builds via tsc -b.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project-reference config for Node-context build tooling (Vite/Playwright config and scripts), separate from the browser app compilation unit.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index for the bundled Aethon agent reference docs (shipped at $AETHON_DOCS_DIR), pointing the model at api.md, components.md, extensions.md, and project-config.md and explaining how to introspect live UI state.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown table mapping each reference doc to its purpose; explicitly instructs the agent to read these on demand rather than from memory.

nodes.name: api.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive runtime API reference for `globalThis.aethon`, covering mutation acknowledgement, A2UI tools, canvas windows, event routing, sessions, shells, editor integration, lifecycle hooks, and introspection helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Large Markdown API reference organized around method-level sections with TypeScript and JSON examples for live UI mutation.

nodes.name: components.md
nodes.summary: Catalog of A2UI primitive components (text, card, button, container, code, table, form, for-each, etc.) and extension-provided composites (layout, sidebar, tab-strip, terminal-panel, dashboards), including prop schemas, the $ref JSON-Pointer data-binding contract, layout-slot contract, and window-event channels.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest doc in the set (50 sections); documents the override-by-type rule and the canonical layout-slot contract used by the renderer.

nodes.name: extensions.md
nodes.summary: Guide to authoring Aethon extensions across the three distribution shapes (loose, project-local, npm-distributed), documenting the extension API surface and eight worked recipes covering themes, sidebar sections, live state updates, layout replacement, native canvas windows, and custom A2UI components.
nodes.languageNotes: Recipe-driven authoring guide with a design-token surface reference and lifecycle/debugging sections.

nodes.name: project-config.md
nodes.summary: Project-local `.aethon` configuration reference documenting workspace startup commands, startup trust behavior, and issue-to-agent prompt templates with supported variables.

nodes.name: text-selection.md
nodes.summary: Defines the app-wide text-selection policy (which surfaces are selectable vs. interactive chrome) and how to implement it in CSS, with guidance for reviewing changes.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Rust crate manifest for the Aethon Tauri shell, defining the package metadata, library and binary targets, Tauri plugin dependencies, platform-specific native dependencies, and optional voice feature stack.
nodes.languageNotes: The manifest uses target-specific dependency tables and optional feature-gated voice dependencies to keep native platform concerns explicit.

nodes.name: Entitlements.plist
nodes.summary: macOS code-signing entitlements granting the app audio-input and speech-recognition capabilities required by the voice-to-text composer feature.
nodes.languageNotes: Apple property-list (plist) XML; these entitlements must be embedded at signing time for the sandboxed app to access the microphone and SFSpeechRecognizer.

nodes.name: Info.plist
nodes.summary: macOS app Info.plist supplying the user-facing privacy usage descriptions (NSMicrophoneUsageDescription, NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription) shown in the OS permission prompts for voice input.
nodes.languageNotes: Apple property-list (plist) XML; macOS refuses microphone/speech access at runtime unless these usage-description strings are present in the bundle.

nodes.name: tauri.conf.json
nodes.summary: Tauri 2 application configuration for the Aethon desktop bundle, wiring build commands, window defaults, bundled resources, icons, macOS plist references, external agent binaries, and updater metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: The bundle section references both generated frontend assets and packaged agent documentation/resources, making this file the native packaging contract.

nodes.name: chrome.css
nodes.summary: Stable stylesheet entry point imported by src/mainApp.tsx that preserves the chrome cascade by importing the split domain stylesheets in the original order.
nodes.languageNotes: CSS entry file uses ordered @import rules as a compatibility layer while domain selectors live in src/styles/chrome/*.css.

nodes.name: themes.css
nodes.summary: Defines Aethon theme palettes and semantic color tokens for built-in light and dark themes, including syntax highlighting, terminal ANSI colors, gradients, state colors, and reduced-motion overrides.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses data-theme selectors, color-scheme declarations, CSS custom properties, color-mix(), gradients, and prefers-reduced-motion to centralize theme behavior.

nodes.name: tokens.css
nodes.summary: Provides theme-agnostic design tokens for typography, spacing, density, radius, z-index, opacity, easing, motion timing, focus rings, elevation shape, viewport sizing, and legacy danger aliasing.
nodes.languageNotes: CSS custom properties establish reusable shape, type, density, and motion primitives consumed by theme and chrome styles.

nodes.name: config-reference.md
nodes.summary: Complete reference for Aethon's ~/.aethon/config.toml, documenting every section ([ui], [agent], [shell], [shortcuts], [voice], [extensions], [updates], [devshell], [server], [guardrails]) plus per-project overrides, value clamps, and environment-variable wiring.
nodes.languageNotes: VitePress reference doc organized as one level-2 section per config table; the 'Clamps and validation' and 'Environment variable wiring' sections mirror the Rust TOML parsing and env-export logic.

nodes.name: keyboard-shortcuts.md
nodes.summary: Reference listing Aethon's default keyboard shortcuts grouped by context (tabs/panel, composer/chat, overlays/surfaces, editor, view, palette prefixes) and explaining how extensions can rebind them.

nodes.name: runtime-api.md
nodes.summary: Website-facing map of the Aethon runtime API, pointing to bundled agent docs while summarizing mutation, frontend runtime, introspection, shell sharing, editor, task, dashboard, and mutation feedback surfaces.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown API reference deliberately distinguishes bundled in-app docs from the website overview.

nodes.name: slash-commands.md
nodes.summary: Reference for built-in and extension-provided slash commands, including command behaviors, extension registration wiring, installation syntax, and picker/palette usage tips.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown reference combines command inventory tables with TypeScript extension examples.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Reference for the aethon-debug slash skill that drives the running dev Tauri app over the TCP eval server, documenting status/send/set-model/screenshot/state/logs actions and dev-only webview globals.

nodes.name: .envrc
nodes.summary: direnv entry point that pins nix-direnv 3.1.1 and activates the project flake devshell automatically on cd, sourcing an optional .envrc.local.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses nix-direnv's cached `use flake` for instant shell re-entry.

nodes.name: copilot-instructions.md
nodes.summary: GitHub Copilot code-review guidance enumerating Aethon's architectural invariants, review priorities, and lockstep-update patterns (keyboard shortcuts, Tauri plugins) that automated reviews must enforce.

nodes.name: 2026-06-03T15-50-03Z__website-index-md.md
nodes.summary: Impeccable design critique of the docs landing page (website/index.md), scoring design health and listing priority issues, anti-pattern verdicts, and persona red flags.

nodes.name: 2026-06-03T16-09-30Z__website-index-md.md
nodes.summary: Follow-up Impeccable re-critique of website/index.md after fixes, recording the post-fix design health score and remaining polish-level issues.

nodes.name: design.json
nodes.summary: Impeccable design-system snapshot capturing extensions, component inventory, and narrative metadata used to track the project's visual design state across critique passes.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Impeccable live-mode configuration declaring which files to watch, insert-before anchors, comment syntax, and CSP-check flag for in-place design iteration.

nodes.name: Project-local .aethon configuration
nodes.summary: Documentation for project-local .aethon configuration, including workspace startup commands, MCP server entries, issue-to-agent templates, fields, and variables.

nodes.name: aethon.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright end-to-end suite for the mocked Aethon app shell, covering boot behavior, chat canvas layout, voice status geometry, queued prompt draining, keyboard/model workflows, and related user-visible regressions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Playwright polling, page.evaluate hooks, and geometry assertions against the mocked Tauri webview runtime.

nodes.name: aethon-harness.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright support harness that injects a mocked Tauri IPC/event environment, fake filesystem/project state, model state, voice provider responses, and agent turn controls for browser-only Aethon E2E tests.
nodes.languageNotes: Large init script mirrors Tauri internals and Aethon debug globals inside the browser page before app startup.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: Flat ESLint configuration for the React frontend, TypeScript bridge, tests, and config files, with strict type-import and unused-variable rules plus scoped relaxations for runtime payload plumbing.
nodes.languageNotes: ESM flat-config composition using typescript-eslint project service and React hook plugins.

nodes.name: clock-demo.ts
nodes.summary: Example Aethon extension that registers a clock component and ticks state once per second, demonstrating the named `register(api)` extension entry shape.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Manifest for the example extension package declaring its module entry and the `aethon` metadata block that points at the frontend module.

nodes.name: frontend.js
nodes.summary: Example extension frontend module shipped to the webview as a string and evaluated with React + extension API in scope; registers a `pulse-card` component with a CSS keyframe animation tied to a state variant.
nodes.languageNotes: Body is eval'd via `new Function("React","extension",code)` — no imports; JSX must be pre-transformed to React.createElement.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Backend entry of the example extension package that registers a sidebar section and an event handler bridging clicks to pi notifications and canvas state updates.

nodes.name: aethon-actions.ts
nodes.summary: Pi extension example adding a Quick Actions sidebar section whose item clicks fire LLM turns through `ctx.pi.prompt`, showing how UI buttons can drive the agent directly.

nodes.name: aethon-clock.ts
nodes.summary: Pi extension example that mutates `globalThis.aethon` UI state on a one-second interval to render a live clock, illustrating the agent-side runtime API.

nodes.name: aethon-counter.ts
nodes.summary: Pi extension example wiring a counter component plus increment/decrement event handlers to demonstrate stateful UI updates from an extension default export.

nodes.name: aethon-files.ts
nodes.summary: Pi extension example that shells out to git to list recently changed files and surfaces them as a sidebar section, demonstrating subprocess use inside an extension.

nodes.name: aethon-sidebar-panel.ts
nodes.summary: Pi extension example registering a custom sidebar panel component to show how extensions extend the chrome surface beyond plain sections.

nodes.name: aethon-theme.ts
nodes.summary: Pi extension example that registers a custom theme via the Aethon runtime API, demonstrating theme registration and activation from an extension.

nodes.name: aethon-types.d.ts
nodes.summary: Ambient TypeScript declarations describing the `globalThis.aethon` runtime API surface (components, themes, sidebar, state, pi context) for authoring typed pi extensions.
nodes.languageNotes: Declaration-only file augmenting globalThis with the extension API contract.

nodes.name: flake.nix
nodes.summary: Nix flake defining Aethon developer shells, pinned Rust toolchain, platform-specific Tauri build inputs, helper commands, treefmt integration, and package/app outputs.
nodes.languageNotes: Large flake-parts configuration with macOS and Linux build workarounds encoded as Nix attributes.

nodes.name: .SRCINFO
nodes.summary: Generated AUR metadata snapshot for the aethon-git package mirroring the PKGBUILD's pkgname, version, dependencies, and source for Arch User Repository indexing.

nodes.name: PKGBUILD
nodes.summary: Arch User Repository build script that builds Aethon from git, declaring runtime dependencies, version derivation, and the package/build steps for distributing the desktop app on Arch Linux.

nodes.name: parseAgentCommandPayload
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw mocked agent command payload into the subset of type, cwd, and model fields asserted by the Playwright tests.

nodes.name: getActiveTurnState
nodes.summary: Reads the app debug state from the page and tolerates transient navigation errors so polling assertions remain stable during app reloads.

nodes.name: getComposerRightOverlayGeometry
nodes.summary: Measures composer overlay, action button, and textarea padding geometry to catch visual overlap regressions in the chat input.

nodes.name: installAethonHarness
nodes.summary: Installs the browser-side Tauri and Aethon test harness before app startup, including IPC mocks, fake filesystem state, event listeners, voice stubs, and agent turn simulation.

nodes.name: waitForAethonReady
nodes.summary: Navigates to the test app and waits until the debug state reports a connected, ready Aethon session.

nodes.name: getInvokeCalls
nodes.summary: Returns captured Tauri invoke calls from the injected harness, retrying across short navigation-context disruptions.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Extension entry that registers the clock component and starts a one-second interval pushing the current time into UI state.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Extension package entry that registers a sidebar section and an onEvent handler bridging item clicks to pi notifications and canvas state writes.

nodes.name: recentGitFiles
nodes.summary: Runs git via execFileSync to collect a de-duplicated list of recently changed files for display in the extension's sidebar.

nodes.name: prepare
nodes.summary: Prepares the AUR source checkout by disabling unsigned updater artifacts, ensuring sourcemaps are omitted from the compiled agent sidecar, and installing Bun dependencies.

nodes.name: package
nodes.summary: Extracts the Tauri-produced Debian bundle into the pacman package root and installs the project license into the Arch package metadata path.

nodes.name: .SRCINFO
nodes.summary: Generated AUR metadata manifest mirroring the PKGBUILD (pkgver 0.4.0, deps, sources) that the Arch User Repository indexes for the aethon package.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated by makepkg --printsrcinfo; never hand-edited.

nodes.name: PKGBUILD
nodes.summary: Arch Linux build recipe for the aethon AUR package, declaring dependencies, source tarball, and the build/package steps that install the Tauri desktop binary.
nodes.languageNotes: Bash-sourced shell script consumed by makepkg; standard PKGBUILD variable conventions (pkgname, depends, build(), package()).

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Short guide for maintaining the AUR package, including how to verify the build in a clean Arch container.

nodes.name: playwright.config.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright end-to-end test configuration that runs the mocked Tauri webview tests against a shared Vite dev server on the configured Vite port.

nodes.name: sync-version.mjs
nodes.summary: Version sync utility that reads the canonical version and rewrites package.json, Cargo.toml, and tauri.conf.json (EOL-preserving) so the three stay aligned; drift fails the build.
nodes.languageNotes: Plain ESM Node script using fs.readFileSync/writeFileSync with EOL detection to avoid line-ending churn.

nodes.name: build.rs
nodes.summary: Cargo build script that compiles the macOS PlatformSpeech Swift static lib, bundles the bun agent sidecar (with pi-coding-agent package.json), provisions the LFM2 resource dir, then runs tauri_build::build.
nodes.languageNotes: Mtime-based incremental rebuild guard (newer_than/newer_recursive) avoids recompiling the sidecar when sources are unchanged.

nodes.name: default.json
nodes.summary: Tauri capability manifest granting the main window its allowlist of plugin permissions (core, fs, dialog, etc.) for IPC.

nodes.name: ic_launcher.xml
nodes.summary: Android adaptive launcher icon descriptor referencing the foreground and background drawable layers.

nodes.name: ic_launcher_background.xml
nodes.summary: Android resource defining the solid background color value used by the adaptive launcher icon.

nodes.name: icon.icns
nodes.summary: Compiled macOS application icon bundle (multi-resolution Apple ICNS asset) shipped with the .app.

nodes.name: PlatformSpeech.swift
nodes.summary: macOS native speech provider exposing C-ABI functions over SFSpeechRecognizer with mic/speech TCC permission preflight, compiled to a static lib and called from Rust via FFI.
nodes.languageNotes: @_cdecl C-callable entry points return heap-allocated C strings freed by aethon_platform_speech_free_string to bridge the Rust/Swift ownership boundary.

nodes.name: main.rs
nodes.summary: Thin binary entry point that disables the Windows console subsystem and delegates to aethon_lib::run() where the Tauri builder lives.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript type definitions for auth profile metadata, provider listings, login lifecycle events, usage data, and the frontend auth-profile UI snapshot.

nodes.name: UpdateBanner.module.css
nodes.summary: Scoped CSS module styling the auto-update banner chrome rendered above the layout by the UpdateBanner component.

nodes.name: useEditorExternalChange.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite for the useEditorExternalChange hook, verifying external-file-change detection, baseline mtime tracking, and dirty-buffer reload prompting via mocked editor buffers.

nodes.name: message-list.zoom-css.test.ts
nodes.summary: CSS contract test that keeps Virtuoso message-list scroll math unzoomed while applying app UI scaling to message rows.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel that re-exports the shell-surface components ShellCanvas, TerminalPanel, and TabStrip for the default-layout extension.

nodes.name: slots.json
nodes.summary: Canonical slot catalogue (header, sidebar, canvas, composer, terminal, status) data source consumed by slots.ts to define the layout contract.

nodes.name: slots.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite validating slots.ts coverage inspection against the workstation.a2ui.json layout, asserting required slots are filled and unknown slots flagged.

nodes.name: slots.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the canonical layout slot catalogue from slots.json and exposes coverage inspection (inspectLayoutSlotCoverage, isKnownSlot) used to validate that a layout payload fills the required slots.

nodes.name: workstation.a2ui.json
nodes.summary: The default workstation A2UI layout payload — the entire app chrome as data — fed to A2UIRenderer, defining the component tree and initial state store rather than hardcoded React.
nodes.languageNotes: Layout-as-payload: this JSON, not App.tsx, defines the default UI; components address shared state via $ref JSON Pointers.

nodes.name: SOURCE.md
nodes.summary: Provenance and licensing note documenting that the bundled file icons are a subset of the Material Icon Theme.

nodes.name: useHostInfo.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite for the useHostInfo hook, verifying local-host info resolution and reaction to host-discovered/host-removed events with mocked persistence and host cache.

nodes.name: scrollbar.test.ts
nodes.summary: CSS contract tests for shared scrollbar sizing, terminal overflow containment, chat overflow behavior, markdown media constraints, and sidebar scroll containment.

nodes.name: titlebar-clearance.test.ts
nodes.summary: CSS contract test for macOS overlay titlebar clearance, keeping sidebar branding and header spacing aligned around traffic-light controls without :has().

nodes.name: newer_than
nodes.summary: Returns whether any agent source file is newer than the built target, gating sidecar recompilation against agent/, package.json, and bun.lockb mtimes.

nodes.name: newer_recursive
nodes.summary: Recursively walks a path comparing each file's modified time against the target mtime to detect any newer source for incremental rebuild decisions.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Build-script entry point: conditionally compiles Swift speech, locates the project root, ensures the agent sidecar and LFM2 resource dir, and invokes tauri_build::build.

nodes.name: compile_platform_speech_swift
nodes.summary: Compiles src-tauri/macos/PlatformSpeech.swift into a static library via xcrun swiftc, emitting cargo link flags and the Swift runtime path for the macOS speech provider.

nodes.name: swift_target
nodes.summary: Maps the Cargo target triple to the macOS Swift target triple expected by swiftc.

nodes.name: command_stdout
nodes.summary: Runs a build helper command and returns stdout, panicking with stderr when the command fails.

nodes.name: ensure_sidecar
nodes.summary: Builds the bun-compiled agent sidecar binary for the target triple and stages the pi-coding-agent package.json alongside it, rebuilding only when sources changed.

nodes.name: aethon_platform_speech_status
nodes.summary: C ABI status entry point that reports current platform speech readiness without requesting permissions.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: aethon_platform_speech_prepare
nodes.summary: C ABI preparation entry point that checks speech readiness and may trigger microphone or speech authorization prompts.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: aethon_platform_speech_transcribe_file
nodes.summary: C-ABI entry point that transcribes an audio file via SFSpeechRecognizer and returns the result as a heap-allocated JSON C string for the Rust caller.

nodes.name: aethon_platform_speech_free_string
nodes.summary: C ABI memory release helper for strings allocated by the Swift speech bridge.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: aethon_platform_speech_cancel
nodes.summary: C ABI cancellation entry point that cancels and clears the active SFSpeech recognition task.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: platformSpeechStatus
nodes.summary: Resolves the combined microphone and speech-recognition authorization status, optionally triggering the OS permission prompt, into a status payload.

nodes.name: tccPreflightFailureMessage
nodes.summary: Validates that Aethon is running from a macOS app bundle with the required privacy usage descriptions before triggering TCC prompts.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: microphoneAuthorizationStatus
nodes.summary: Reads or requests microphone permission synchronously for the Rust-facing status and preparation calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: speechAuthorizationStatus
nodes.summary: Reads or requests Apple Speech recognition permission synchronously for status and preparation calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: sfSpeechStatus
nodes.summary: Checks the locale-specific SFSpeechRecognizer availability and returns a ready or unavailable bridge status.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: transcribeWithSFSpeech
nodes.summary: Runs an SFSpeechURLRecognitionRequest against the given audio URL, blocking on a semaphore until the recognizer yields a final transcription or error.

nodes.name: friendlySFSpeechErrorMessage
nodes.summary: Maps common Apple speech error codes to user-facing retry guidance while preserving localized descriptions for unknown errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: writeStatus
nodes.summary: Writes a Swift speech status struct into optional C output pointers for the Rust FFI caller.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: writeTranscription
nodes.summary: Writes transcription status, engine, text, and message pointers into the C output parameters expected by Rust.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: transcriptionSuccess
nodes.summary: Constructs a successful transcription payload with copied transcript text and engine label.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: transcriptionError
nodes.summary: Constructs an error transcription payload with a status code, engine id, and copied message string.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: microphonePermissionMessage
nodes.summary: Translates AVFoundation microphone authorization states into concise status messages.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: speechPermissionMessage
nodes.summary: Translates SFSpeech authorization states into concise status messages.
nodes.languageNotes: Exported @_cdecl functions form the Rust FFI surface; private helpers keep Apple Speech authorization and callback handling synchronous for that boundary.

nodes.name: setup
nodes.summary: Builds a rendered hook fixture with mocked dirty state, reload callback, and baseline mtime waiting for external-change tests.

nodes.name: findWorkstationComponent
nodes.summary: Traverses the workstation A2UI payload tree to locate a component by id for slot-contract assertions.

nodes.name: isKnownSlot
nodes.summary: Checks whether a string is one of the documented canonical layout slot names.

nodes.name: inspectLayoutSlotCoverage
nodes.summary: Walks an A2UI layout payload to report which canonical slots are filled, which are unknown, and whether all required slots are covered.

nodes.name: vcs-badges.test.ts
nodes.summary: Verifies that merged pull-request badges in source-control and header chrome use the same success colors as workspace merged badges while neutral badges remain visually distinct.

nodes.name: setup.ts
nodes.summary: Global Vitest setup file that mocks the Tauri core/event APIs and monaco-editor so App-tier code under jsdom/node does not invoke the real runtime.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses vi.mock for @tauri-apps/api and monaco-editor to stub browser/native globals missing under jsdom.

nodes.name: voiceHotkeys.ts
nodes.summary: Dependency-free voice hotkey constants and platform detection helpers, shared by the voice hook and settings slice to avoid a circular import.

nodes.name: isMacPlatform
nodes.summary: Detects whether the runtime is macOS by testing navigator.platform and userAgent, guarding against an undefined navigator.

nodes.name: getDefaultHoldHotkey
nodes.summary: Returns the platform-aware default hold-to-talk key, AltRight on macOS and null elsewhere to avoid clashing with AltGr text entry.

nodes.name: vite-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: Ambient type-reference declaration pulling in Vite's client types for the frontend TypeScript project.

nodes.name: highlight.worker.ts
nodes.summary: Web Worker that lazily loads a Shiki highlighter core and grammars, highlights code to sanitized HAST, and supports on-demand grammar registration from the main thread.
nodes.languageNotes: Lazy dynamic import() of Shiki core + Oniguruma engine, with per-language load/fail caches and a structural-safety filter over the HAST before serializing to HTML.

nodes.name: getHighlighter
nodes.summary: Memoizes creation of the Shiki highlighter core, dynamically importing the WASM Oniguruma engine and base theme exactly once.

nodes.name: ensureLang
nodes.summary: Ensures a language grammar is loaded into the highlighter, deduplicating in-flight registrations and tracking loaded and permanently-failed languages.

nodes.name: isStructurallySafe
nodes.summary: Recursively validates a HAST node against an allow-list of properties to prevent unsafe attributes from surviving serialization.

nodes.name: findCodeElement
nodes.summary: Walks the highlighter's HAST output to locate the inner code element holding the tokenized line spans.

nodes.name: highlight
nodes.summary: Highlights a code string for a given language to HAST, applies the structural-safety filter, strips line separators, and serializes to HTML.

nodes.name: loadGrammar
nodes.summary: Loads a caller-supplied grammar into the highlighter, updating the loaded/failed caches and warning on failure.

nodes.name: registerGrammar
nodes.summary: Registers an externally-provided grammar on demand, deduplicating concurrent registrations via a per-language promise map.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite + Vitest configuration for the frontend: react plugin, strict fixed dev port, .direnv/target watch exclusions, and node-environment unit test settings with v8 coverage.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports defineConfig from vitest/config (not vite) so the test field is on the schema; pins strictPort to fail loudly when the dev wrapper's chosen port is busy.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the Aethon documentation site metadata, navigation, sidebars, social links, local search, and release-version menu for the VitePress GitHub Pages build.

nodes.name: index.ts

nodes.name: hangWarn.ts
nodes.summary: Hang-warning subsystem that arms, refreshes, and clears per-tab timers, surfacing a notification when an agent prompt stalls without activity.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses requestAnimationFrame-batched refresh flushing plus per-tab timer maps held in React refs to coalesce frequent bridge-message activity into hang-warn arming.

nodes.name: clearHangWarn
nodes.summary: Clears a tab's hang-warn timer and active state and dismisses its notification.

nodes.name: armHangWarn
nodes.summary: Schedules a hang-warn timer for a tab that, on fire, marks it active and pushes a hang-warning notification.

nodes.name: refreshHangWarn
nodes.summary: Re-arms or clears a tab's hang warning in response to fresh activity, dismissing the notification if one is active.

nodes.name: scheduleHangWarnRefresh
nodes.summary: Queues a tab's hang-warn refresh into the rAF-batched flush, dismissing any active notification immediately.

nodes.name: bridgeMessageRefreshesHangWarn
nodes.summary: Predicate that classifies whether a bridge message represents activity that should refresh a tab's hang warning.

nodes.name: refreshHangWarnForBridgeMessage
nodes.summary: Entry point that refreshes a tab's hang warning when an incoming bridge message qualifies as activity.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel and registry that assembles every bridge message handler into the bridgeMessageHandlers map and re-exports shared handler types.
nodes.languageNotes: Aggregates 50+ individual handler modules into a single dispatch table keyed by bridge message type.

nodes.name: layoutPatch.test.ts

nodes.name: layoutPatch.ts
nodes.summary: Bridge handler that applies a partial layout mutation to the current A2UI layout via layoutPatch and acks the mutation.

nodes.name: handleLayoutPatch

nodes.name: layoutSet.ts

nodes.name: handleLayoutSet
nodes.summary: Validates the incoming layout array, sets it as the active layout, deep-merges optional state, and acks the mutation.

nodes.name: modelChanged.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit test verifying the model-changed handler updates the tab model, records the project default, and recomputes the model picker.

nodes.name: modelChanged.ts
nodes.summary: Bridge handler that records an agent-reported model change onto the tab, persists it as the project default, and refreshes the model picker.

nodes.name: handleModelChanged
nodes.summary: Updates the tab's model, records it as the project's preferred model, and recomputes the model-picker state.

nodes.name: nativeSlashResult.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit test covering the native-slash-result handler's message append, status flags, and notification side effects.

nodes.name: nativeSlashResult.ts
nodes.summary: Bridge handler that renders the result of a native slash command into the chat stream, updating tab status and persisting the message.

nodes.name: handleNativeSlashResult

nodes.name: nativeWindowQuery.test.ts
nodes.summary: Extensive unit test suite exercising every routed operation of the native-window-query handler (open, close, set state, canvas readiness, shell cleanup) with mocked Tauri IPC.

nodes.name: nativeWindowQuery.ts
nodes.summary: Bridge handler implementing the full native-window query surface: opening/closing native canvas windows, persisting records, syncing window state, and cleaning up owned terminal shells.
nodes.languageNotes: Large multi-operation router whose handleNativeWindowQuery delegates to an inner route() switch across ~15 native-window operations, each invoking Tauri commands and reconciling an in-memory record map against frontend state.

nodes.name: waitForNativeCanvasReady
nodes.summary: Resolves once the native canvas window with the given id emits its ready event, with a timeout fallback and listener cleanup.

nodes.name: getRecord
nodes.summary: Returns a native-window record from the in-memory ref or fetches and caches it from the backend, syncing window state.

nodes.name: persistRecord
nodes.summary: Persists a native-window record to the backend, updates the in-memory ref, and syncs window state.

nodes.name: cleanupOwnedTerminalShells
nodes.summary: Closes terminal shells owned by a closing native window and prunes the corresponding shell tabs from state.

nodes.name: handleNativeWindowQuery
nodes.summary: Dispatches native-window query operations through an inner route() switch and acks the mutation with the result or an error.

nodes.name: notice.test.ts

nodes.name: notice.ts

nodes.name: handleNotice

nodes.name: notification.test.ts

nodes.name: notification.ts

nodes.name: handleNotification

nodes.name: notificationDismiss.test.ts

nodes.name: activeAgentTab

nodes.name: visibleChangedDirs

nodes.name: file-tree.tsx
